steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both functions have base $\gt 1$ and $x \geq 0$, so both are increasing - minimum occurs at $x = 0$. The question asks whether that minimum value appears as a visible constant or coefficient in the given equation (not whether you can compute it).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A - ‘I only’: $f(0) = 16(1.3)^5$. That product does not appear as a single constant in $f(x) = 16(1.3)^{x+5}$ - you must compute it. Incorrect
  • Choice B - ‘II only’: $g(0) = 16(1.09)(1.3)^3$. Similarly, that product is not written as a single constant in $g(x) = 16(1.09)(1.3)^{x+3}$. Incorrect
  • Choice C - ‘I and II’: Neither displays its minimum directly - both require multiplying separate factors. Incorrect
  • Choice D - ‘Neither I nor II’: Correct. Neither equation shows the minimum as an explicit constant - both require evaluating a product of factors to find it. Correct
That matches choice D - D is correct.
